title: Renal Embryology: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Renal Embryology
Identify key diagnostic features of Renal Embryology
Apply clinical reasoning to Renal Embryology management
Recognize common pitfalls in Renal Embryology

sections.title: Overview of Renal Embryology
sections.content:
**Renal Embryology**

Pronephros → mesonephros → metanephros development, ureteric bud and metanephric mesenchyme interaction, renal anomalies

**Key Points:**
• Ureteric bud (from mesonephric duct) → collecting system (collecting ducts, calyces, renal pelvis, ureter)
• Metanephric mesenchyme → nephrons (glomerulus through DCT)
• Reciprocal induction between ureteric bud and mesenchyme

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Ureteric bud (from mesonephric duct) → collecting system (collecting ducts, calyces, renal pelvis, ureter)
• Metanephric mesenchyme → nephrons (glomerulus through DCT)

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Reciprocal induction between ureteric bud and mesenchyme
• Common anomalies: horseshoe kidney, pelvic kidney, duplex collecting system, renal agenesis
• Potter sequence: bilateral renal agenesis → oligohydramnios → pulmonary hypoplasia + limb/facial compression

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Renal Embryology:**

• Reciprocal induction between ureteric bud and mesenchyme
• Common anomalies: horseshoe kidney, pelvic kidney, duplex collecting system, renal agenesis
• Potter sequence: bilateral renal agenesis → oligohydramnios → pulmonary hypoplasia + limb/facial compression

mcqs.question: Which of the following is MOST characteristic of Renal Embryology?

options.text: Ureteric bud (from mesonephric duct) → collecting system (collecting ducts, calyces, renal pelvis, ureter)
options.explanation: Ureteric bud (from mesonephric duct) → collecting system (collecting ducts, calyces, renal pelvis, ureter)

options.text: Requires invasive testing in all cases

mcqs.question: What is the KEY diagnostic or management consideration for Renal Embryology?

options.text: Potter sequence: bilateral renal agenesis → oligohydramnios → pulmonary hypoplasia + limb/facial compression
options.explanation: Potter sequence: bilateral renal agenesis → oligohydramnios → pulmonary hypoplasia + limb/facial compression

options.text: Immediate surgical intervention in all cases
